name: Adyghe
notes: Adyghe (адыгабзэ, West Circassian) — Northwest Caucasian language of the Republic of Adygea and the Circassian diaspora. Written in a Cyrillic-based alphabet (an Arabic-script orthography is used by some diaspora communities). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: the Adyghe Cyrillic alphabet encodes a very large consonant inventory (ejectives, labialised and pharyngealised series) through multi-character digraphs and trigraphs (е.g. ку, къу, гъу, лӏ, шӏу), which requires dedicated research to map reliably. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the standard Adyghe Cyrillic orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode adyg1241), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode adyg1241.

ancestors.notes: Abkhaz-Adyge — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for West Circassian.
